title: Gate 29: The Abysmal
description: You are built for full commitment. When your gut says YES to something, you can go deeper and sustain longer than almost anyone. The trap is saying yes to things your gut isn't truly in — exhaustion follows every half-hearted commitment.
learningGoal: The viewer understands what Gate 29 (The Abysmal — Sacred commitment to life's depths) means in their daily life, in plain terms.
keyPoints: You are built for full commitment.
When your gut says YES to something, you can go deeper and sustain longer than almost anyone.
The trap is saying yes to things your gut isn't truly in
forbiddenClaims: a gate guarantees a talent you do not have to develop
gates rank people as better or worse
an activated gate forces a fixed behaviour

variants.angleNote: THE GIFT — what this gives you. Recognition, the quiet power of it. Make them feel seen.
variants.hook: Gate 29: The Abysmal — the part most people miss.
variants.participationPrompt: Does this land? Drop a ✦ if this is you.

variants.angleNote: THE SHADOW — the not-self trap, the misalignment signal to watch for. Honest, never fearful.
variants.hook: Gate 29: The Abysmal — the trap nobody warns you about.
variants.participationPrompt: Caught yourself here before? Tell me where.

variants.angleNote: THE PRACTICE — one concrete move for today. Action-oriented, motion-forward, doable now.
variants.hook: Gate 29: The Abysmal — try this today.
variants.participationPrompt: Try it today. Come back and tell me what shifted.

variants.angleNote: THE MIRROR — how this shows up with other people. The relational lens.
variants.hook: Gate 29: The Abysmal — why it changes every room you enter.
variants.participationPrompt: Who in your life does this explain? Tag the feeling.

toneNotes: Oracle/sage register: warm, recognition-based, unhurried, mythic but grounded. Speak directly to the viewer. No jargon, no hype, no fear.

output_metadata.title: Gate 29: The Abysmal — Your Energy Blueprint
output_metadata.description: You are built for full commitment. When your gut says YES to something, you can go deeper and sustain longer than almost anyone. The trap is saying yes to things your gut isn't truly in — exhaustion follows every half-hearted commitment.
